steps.title: Identify the counterargument
steps.content:
The passage signals a direct challenge to the invasive/native framework when Camacho and McLachlan are introduced with the word But.

They observe that climate change shifts species ranges and creates new habitats—facts that destabilize the assumption that current classifications remain stable and valid.

steps.title: Apply the climate challenge to the conclusion
steps.content:
C follows directly: if climate reshapes where species can live, the fixed invasive/native classifications become unreliable and must be reevaluated.

  • A hedges about partial usefulness
  • B invents a false history
  • D addresses coordination rather than reclassification
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
